ddls:
CREATE TABLE "counties" (
  "county_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each county */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the county */,
  "state_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the state the county belongs to */,
  "geoid" TEXT /* Geographic identifier for the county */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the county */,
  "area" REAL /* Total area of the county in square miles */,
  "population_density" REAL /* Population density (population per square mile) */,
  "date_updated" TEXT /* Last date the county data was updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("county_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_counties_state_id FOREIGN KEY ("state_id") REFERENCES states ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "states" (
  "state_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each state */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the state */,
  "abbreviation" TEXT /* Abbreviation for the state (e.g., VA, CA) */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the state */,
  "area" REAL /* Total area of the state in square miles */,
  "population_density" REAL /* Population density (population per square mile) */,
  "date_updated" TEXT /* Last date the state data was updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographic_datasets" (
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dataset */,
  "county_id" INTEGER /* ID of the county the data belongs to */,
  "estimate" INTEGER /* Estimated demographic value (e.g., population count) */,
  "margin_of_error" INTEGER /* Margin of error for the estimated value */,
  "order" INTEGER /* Order of the demographic metric */,
  "line_type" TEXT /* Type of demographic data (e.g., T, S, D) */,
  "tenure" TEXT /* Type of tenure (e.g., All, Owner, Renter) */,
  "overcrowding" TEXT /* Overcrowding status (e.g., All, No overcrowding) */,
  "household_income" TEXT /* Household income range (e.g., All, 30% AMI or less) */,
  "family_structure" TEXT /* Family structure (e.g., All, 1 family household) */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the demographic data (e.g., Census Bureau, ACS) */,
  "collection_method" TEXT /* Method used to collect the data (e.g., survey, administrative records) */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the data was collected */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version number of the dataset */,
  "date_created" TEXT /* Date the dataset was created */,
  "date_updated" TEXT /* Last date the dataset was updated */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the dataset */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographic_datasets_overcrowding FOREIGN KEY ("overcrowding") REFERENCES overcrowding ("overcrowding_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographic_datasets_tenure FOREIGN KEY ("tenure") REFERENCES tenures ("tenure_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographic_datasets_family_structure FOREIGN KEY ("family_structure") REFERENCES family_structures ("family_structure_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographic_datasets_county_id FOREIGN KEY ("county_id") REFERENCES counties ("county_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographic_datasets_household_income FOREIGN KEY ("household_income") REFERENCES household_incomes ("household_income_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographic_datasets_line_type FOREIGN KEY ("line_type") REFERENCES line_types ("line_type_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "line_types" (
  "line_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each line type */,
  "line_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the line type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the line type */,
  "date_updated" TEXT /* Last date the line type data was updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("line_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tenures" (
  "tenure_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tenure */,
  "tenure_name" TEXT /* Name of the tenure */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the tenure */,
  "date_updated" TEXT /* Last date the tenure data was updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tenure_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "overcrowding" (
  "overcrowding_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each overcrowding status */,
  "overcrowding_name" TEXT /* Name of the overcrowding status */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the overcrowding status */,
  "date_updated" TEXT /* Last date the overcrowding data was updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("overcrowding_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "household_incomes" (
  "household_income_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each household income range */,
  "household_income_name" TEXT /* Name of the household income range */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the household income range */,
  "date_updated" TEXT /* Last date the household income data was updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("household_income_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "family_structures" (
  "family_structure_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each family structure */,
  "family_structure_name" TEXT /* Name of the family structure */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the family structure */,
  "date_updated" TEXT /* Last date the family structure data was updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("family_structure_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "date_joined" TEXT /* Date the user joined the platform */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last date the user logged in */,
  "status" TEXT /* User status (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the dataset */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the dataset was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the dataset */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used to access the dataset */,
  "date_updated" TEXT /* Last date the access log was updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES demographic_datasets ("dataset_id")
)
